figures.caption: Figure 2 | Overview of ClawGUI-RL, consisting of an RL Infrastructure and a Real & Virtual Environment backend. The Environment Manager orchestrates multi-task parallel rollouts across real devices and Android emulators, with built-in health checking, crash recovery, and spare server rotation. Task evaluation combines system-level verification with MLLM-as-judge to provide robust reward signals for both virtual and real device training.

Figure 4 | Overview of ClawGUI-Agent, where users issue natural language instructions through 12+ chat platforms and the server side executes tasks via a message-driven agent loop with persistent memory and skills, controlling both virtual and real devices across phone, web browser, and desktop.

Table 3 | Reproduction results across GUI grounding benchmarks. Green bold indicates successful reproduction (|Δ| ≤ 2% or reproduced ≥ official); red indicates a gap exceeding the threshold; - indicates no official baseline. Benchmark abbreviations: SS-Pro = ScreenSpot-Pro, SS-V2 = ScreenSpot-V2, UIV = UIVision, MMB = MMBench-GUI, OSW-G = OSWorld-G. Closed-source models are evaluated on ScreenSpot-Pro only via a two-stage Zoom paradigm.
Table 1 | Comparison of models on GUI-Only (117 tasks) benchmark.

Figure 3 | Overview of ClawGUI-Eval, featuring a standardized Infer → Judge → Metric pipeline across 6 benchmarks and 11+ models. Reproduced results are compared against official baselines across five benchmarks, achieving a 95.8% overall reproduction rate. The full pipeline can be triggered via a single natural language command through ClawGUI-Agent (OpenClaw-GUI).
